reasoning: The Lean definition `R n := MvPolynomial (Fin n) ℂ ⧸ Ideal.span {∑ i, X i ^ 2}` correctly represents ℂ[x_1,...,x_n]/(x_1²+...+x_n²). The conclusion `∃ (h : IsDomain (R n)), UniqueFactorizationMonoid (R n)` is logically equivalent to `IsDomain (R n) ∧ UniqueFactorizationMonoid (R n)` since the body doesn't depend on h. This correctly captures "UFD" because in Mathlib, `UniqueFactorizationMonoid` extends `IsCancelMulZero` but not `Nontrivial`, so `IsDomain` is required separately to assert the full "integral domain + unique factorization" meaning of UFD. The hypothesis `n ≥ 5` matches the informal statement exactly. The universal quantification over n is correctly represented.
